hypothesis: If ML surrogates (eqV2 or eSEN) are used to initialize and restart SSNEB calculations to generate ML-converged minimum energy pathways for solid-state phase transitions in CsPbI3 and TiO2, then the ML-assisted SSNEB will require fewer iterations and less computational time to converge to the MEPs and transition states than a full VASP-only SSNEB, while achieving comparable energy barriers and pathways.
